CL:0000084.name: T cell
CL:0000084.clDescription: A type of lymphocyte whose defining characteristic is the expression of a T cell receptor complex.

CL:0000623.name: natural killer cell
CL:0000623.clDescription: A lymphocyte that can spontaneously kill a variety of target cells without prior antigenic activation via germline encoded activation receptors and also regulate immune responses via cytokine release and direct contact with other cells.

CL:0000236.name: B cell
CL:0000236.clDescription: A lymphocyte of B lineage that is capable of B cell mediated immunity.

CL:0000235.name: macrophage
CL:0000235.clDescription: A mononuclear phagocyte present in variety of tissues, typically differentiated from monocytes, capable of phagocytosing a variety of extracellular particulate material, including immune complexes, microorganisms, and dead cells.

CL:0000763.name: myeloid cell
CL:0000763.clDescription: A cell of the monocyte, granulocyte, mast cell, megakaryocyte, or erythroid lineage.

CL:0000057.name: fibroblast
CL:0000057.clDescription: A connective tissue cell which secretes an extracellular matrix rich in collagen and other macromolecules. Flattened and irregular in outline with branching processes; appear fusiform or spindle-shaped.

CL:0000097.name: mast cell
CL:0000097.clDescription: A cell that is found in almost all tissues containing numerous basophilic granules and capable of releasing large amounts of histamine and heparin upon activation. Progenitors leave bone marrow and mature in connective and mucosal tissue. Mature mast cells are found in all tissues, except the bloodstream. Their phenotype is CD117-high, CD123-negative, CD193-positive, CD200R3-positive, and FceRI-high. Stem-cell factor (KIT-ligand; SCF) is the main controlling signal of their survival and development.

CL:0000148.name: melanocyte
CL:0000148.clDescription: A pigment cell derived from the neural crest. Contains melanin-filled pigment granules, which gives a brown to black appearance.

CL:0000066.name: epithelial cell
CL:0000066.clDescription: A cell that is usually found in a two-dimensional sheet with a free surface. The cell has a cytoskeleton that allows for tight cell to cell contact and for cell polarity where apical part is directed towards the lumen and the basal part to the basal lamina.

CL:0000115.name: endothelial cell
CL:0000115.clDescription: An endothelial cell comprises the outermost layer or lining of anatomical structures and can be squamous or cuboidal. In mammals, endothelial cell has vimentin filaments and is derived from the mesoderm.
